refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-003 Refined: The NGHE solution shall enable operators and tele-operators to achieve a measured energy efficiency of energy_per_ton ≤ E_target (customer-defined threshold) over the agreed evaluation period, through integrated monitoring, control optimization, and real-time feedback mechanisms.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is outcome-focused but lacks explicit linkage to the mechanisms (monitoring, control, feedback) that enable efficiency achievement. Refinement clarifies that energy efficiency is not a passive property but an active capability requiring measurement, visibility, and operator decision support. This aligns with the high safety criticality constraint by ensuring operators have visibility into energy consumption patterns.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure energy_per_ton = total_energy_used / total_ton_moved over the agreed evaluation period (e.g., 30-day operational window). Verify that measured value ≤ E_target. Collect telemetry from Vehicle power systems and cross-validate with site-level energy metering. Confirm operator feedback mechanisms are functional and used.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh) and work output (tons moved) from Vehicle subsystems.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time energy efficiency metrics (energy_per_ton) to operators and tele-operators via Telemetry interface.
CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall support operator control strategies (e.g., load optimization, route planning, idle reduction) that minimize energy_per_ton.

CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall alert Safety Manager and Maintenance when energy_per_ton deviates significantly from baseline, indicating potential mechanical degradation or operational inefficiency.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement energy monitoring and reporting subsystem within Vehicle; provide aggregated energy_per_ton KPI via Telemetry to operator dashboards; no active control optimization.

DP-B: Integrate energy monitoring (Vehicle), real-time efficiency feedback (Telemetry), operator advisory system (Teleoperation), and predictive maintenance alerts (Safety Services) to form a closed-loop energy optimization ecosystem.

DP-C: Centralize all energy efficiency logic in a dedicated Energy Management Module external to Vehicle; Vehicle reports raw power telemetry; external module computes KPI and sends control recommendations back to Vehicle via Tasking interface.

DP-D: Distribute energy monitoring across Vehicle (power measurement), Teleoperation (operator feedback and advisory), and Safety Services (anomaly detection and alerts); coordinate via Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Integrate energy monitoring (Vehicle), real-time efficiency feedback (Telemetry), operator advisory system (Teleoperation), and predictive maintenance alerts (Safety Services) to form a closed-loop energy optimization ecosystem.

potential_sources: Vehicle energy monitoring must be synchronized with Telemetry reporting cadence to avoid stale KPI data.
Teleoperation advisory system must respond to Telemetry efficiency signals within operator reaction time (< 2 sec latency assumed).
Safety Services anomaly detection must not conflict with operator control decisions; escalation protocol required.
Network Link reliability is critical; loss of Telemetry or SafetyAlert could degrade feedback loop.
coupling_check.notes: DP-B exhibits controlled coupling: each subsystem (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) has a clear role in the closed-loop. Coupling is managed through well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking). This is preferable to DP-C (external centralization, which creates a single point of failure) or DP-D (distributed logic, which risks inconsistent KPI computation). DP-A is rejected because it lacks operator advisory and maintenance integration, limiting effectiveness.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle power measurement subsystem is accurate to ±5% and available continuously.
Assumption: Telemetry bandwidth supports real-time (≤ 1 sec) energy_per_ton updates.
Assumption: Operator advisory system in Teleoperation can be trained/configured to recommend efficiency-improving actions without conflicting with safety or productivity goals.
Risk: If Vehicle power measurement fails, energy_per_ton KPI becomes unreliable; mitigation: redundant sensors or cross-validation with site-level metering.
Risk: Operator may ignore advisory signals if they conflict with short-term productivity pressure; mitigation: Safety Manager oversight and incentive alignment.
Risk: Network latency or packet loss could break the feedback loop; mitigation: graceful degradation (fall back to operator manual control) and Network Link redundancy.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-B is sound. Recommend explicit specification of: (1) energy measurement accuracy and availability SLA for Vehicle subsystem, (2) Telemetry update frequency and latency budget, (3) operator advisory decision logic and conflict resolution with safety constraints, (4) Network Link redundancy and failover strategy.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution IM is the top-level integration boundary that orchestrates energy efficiency across Vehicle (power measurement and control), Teleoperation (operator advisory), and Safety Services (anomaly detection and alerts). It owns the energy_per_ton KPI computation logic, the closed-loop feedback coordination, and the interface contracts between subsystems.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits real-time energy_per_ton KPI, power consumption (kW), work output (tons/hour), and efficiency trend data to operator dashboards and monitoring systems. Frequency: ≤ 1 sec. Consumers: Site Manager, Equipment Operator, Tele-operator, Maintenance/Quality.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits alerts when energy_per_ton deviates > threshold from baseline (indicating mechanical degradation or operational anomaly). Severity levels: Warning, Critical. Consumers: Safety Manager, Maintenance/Quality.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives operator commands and control directives (e.g., load optimization mode, route planning parameters) from Teleoperation. These inform the energy optimization strategy within Vehicle and Teleoperation advisory logic.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution depends on Network Link for reliable, low-latency communication of Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and Tasking. Network Link is a critical dependency for closed-loop operation.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level integration boundary. It owns the energy efficiency KPI definition, closed-loop coordination logic, and interface contracts. Minimal diffusion: only Network Link dependency is external. This is the correct architectural choice for a system-level FR.

Vehicle contains power measurement and control subsystems, which are essential to DP-B. However, Vehicle alone cannot implement the full closed-loop: it lacks Teleoperation advisory logic and Safety Services anomaly detection. Assigning CR-NGHE-003 to Vehicle would fragment the energy efficiency capability across multiple modules, violating cohesion. Vehicle should be a child IM under NGHE Solution.

Teleoperation contains operator advisory and feedback mechanisms, which are part of DP-B. However, Teleoperation does not own Vehicle power measurement or Safety Services anomaly detection. Assigning CR-NGHE-003 to Teleoperation would create diffusion across Vehicle and Safety Services, violating cohesion. Teleoperation should be a child IM under NGHE Solution.

Safety Services contains anomaly detection and alerting, which is part of DP-B. However, Safety Services does not own Vehicle power measurement or Teleoperation operator advisory. Assigning CR-NGHE-003 to Safety Services would create significant diffusion. Safety Services should be a child IM under NGHE Solution.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary because: (1) it owns the energy_per_ton KPI definition and computation logic, which is the core of CR-NGHE-003; (2) it orchestrates the closed-loop feedback across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; (3) it manages the interface contracts (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking, Network Link) that enable energy efficiency; (4) assigning to any child module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) would fragment the capability and violate cohesion.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution is a high-level integration boundary; detailed energy optimization algorithms may be distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Mitigation: Define clear ownership of each algorithm component (e.g., Vehicle owns power measurement and load optimization, Teleoperation owns operator advisory, Safety Services owns anomaly detection) and document the interface contracts.

Risk: Network Link is external to NGHE Solution; loss of Network Link breaks the closed-loop. Mitigation: Design graceful degradation (Vehicle can operate in local optimization mode if Network Link fails) and implement Network Link redundancy.

Risk: Operator may override energy efficiency recommendations for short-term productivity; NGHE Solution must balance efficiency with safety and productivity. Mitigation: Define clear priority rules (safety > productivity > efficiency) and implement Safety Manager oversight.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary. Recommend: (1) define child IMs for Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services with explicit ownership of energy efficiency sub-functions; (2) document the interface contracts (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking) with latency, bandwidth, and reliability SLAs; (3) specify the energy_per_ton KPI computation logic and its location (likely in NGHE Solution or Vehicle, with aggregation in NGHE Solution); (4) define the closed-loop feedback coordination protocol and conflict resolution rules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: CR-NGHE-003 Refined FR clearly maps to DP-B: the FR requires energy efficiency achievement through integrated monitoring, control optimization, and feedback; DP-B provides exactly this through Vehicle monitoring, Teleoperation advisory, and Safety Services alerts. The mapping is non-trivial (not a 1:1 restatement) and addresses the high safety criticality by ensuring operator visibility and Safety Manager oversight. No independence violations detected.

diffusion_risks: DP-B requires coordination across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. If these modules have weak interface contracts or unclear ownership, diffusion risk is high. Mitigation: Define explicit interface SLAs (latency, bandwidth, reliability) and ownership rules for each energy efficiency sub-function.
Network Link is a critical dependency for closed-loop operation. If Network Link is unreliable, the feedback loop breaks. Mitigation: Design graceful degradation and implement redundancy.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution IM correctly contains the energy efficiency closed-loop coordination. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are child IMs that implement specific sub-functions (power measurement, operator advisory, anomaly detection) under NGHE Solution orchestration. This is a sound hierarchical decomposition.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: CR-NGHE-003 Refined FR is verifiable: measure energy_per_ton over the agreed evaluation period and compare to E_target. Telemetry interface provides real-time KPI data. Verification requires: (1) accurate Vehicle power measurement, (2) reliable Telemetry reporting, (3) operator feedback on advisory system usage, (4) Safety Manager oversight of anomalies. All are feasible within NGHE Solution IM.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: CR-NGHE-003 is allocated to NGHE Solution IM with clear sub-allocations to Vehicle (power measurement and control), Teleoperation (operator advisory), and Safety Services (anomaly detection). Each sub-allocation is traceable to a sub-FR (CR-NGHE-003.1 through 003.4). Allocation is clear and hierarchical.

recommended_revisions: Specify energy measurement accuracy and availability SLA for Vehicle subsystem (e.g., ±5% accuracy, 99.9% availability).
Specify Telemetry update frequency and latency budget (e.g., ≤ 1 sec latency, ≥ 1 Hz frequency).
Define operator advisory decision logic in Teleoperation and conflict resolution with safety constraints.
Specify Network Link redundancy and failover strategy to ensure closed-loop reliability.
Define the energy_per_ton KPI computation logic and its location (Vehicle or NGHE Solution).
Specify the evaluation period and E_target value (customer-defined; ensure this is captured in requirements traceability).

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-003 Refined FR is well-formed and maps to a sound DP (closed-loop energy optimization ecosystem). The IM assignment to NGHE Solution is correct and exhibits high containment (95%) with minimal diffusion (5%). However, the FR is not atomic: it decomposes into four sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-003.1 through 003.4) that address distinct concerns (measurement, feedback, control, and alerting). To ensure complete and rigorous design, we must decompose to the next level and refine each sub-FR with its own DP and IM assignment. This is a ZIGZAG_DOWN decision: proceed to the next decomposition level.
decision.justification_detail: The refined FR explicitly lists sub-FRs that are not yet decomposed. Each sub-FR requires its own DP and IM assignment to ensure architectural clarity and ownership boundaries. For example, CR-NGHE-003.1 (measurement) should map to Vehicle power measurement subsystem; CR-NGHE-003.2 (feedback) should map to Telemetry interface and operator dashboard; CR-NGHE-003.3 (control) should map to Vehicle control optimization and Teleoperation advisory; CR-NGHE-003.4 (alerting) should map to Safety Services anomaly detection. Proceeding to the next level will clarify these allocations and reduce architectural risk.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh) and work output (tons moved) from Vehicle subsystems.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time energy efficiency metrics (energy_per_ton) to operators and tele-operators via Telemetry interface.
CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall support operator control strategies (e.g., load optimization, route planning, idle reduction) that minimize energy_per_ton.

CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall alert Safety Manager and Maintenance when energy_per_ton deviates significantly from baseline, indicating potential mechanical degradation or operational inefficiency.

assumptions: Vehicle power measurement subsystem is accurate to ±5% and available continuously.
Telemetry bandwidth supports real-time (≤ 1 sec) energy_per_ton updates.
Operator advisory system can be trained to recommend efficiency-improving actions without conflicting with safety or productivity goals.
Network Link is reliable enough to support closed-loop operation; redundancy is available if needed.
Customer-defined E_target is achievable with the NGHE solution and operator best practices.
Evaluation period is long enough (e.g., 30 days) to capture representative operational patterns and exclude anomalies.
open_risks: Vehicle power measurement failure could render energy_per_ton KPI unreliable; mitigation: redundant sensors or cross-validation with site-level metering.
Operator may ignore advisory signals if they conflict with short-term productivity pressure; mitigation: Safety Manager oversight and incentive alignment.
Network latency or packet loss could break the feedback loop; mitigation: graceful degradation and Network Link redundancy.
Energy efficiency may conflict with other KPIs (e.g., cycle time, safety); mitigation: define clear priority rules and trade-off analysis.
Maintenance degradation (e.g., worn hydraulics) may increase energy_per_ton over time; mitigation: predictive maintenance alerts and baseline recalibration.
evidence_needed: Vehicle power measurement specification and validation data (accuracy, availability, sensor redundancy).
Telemetry interface specification (latency, bandwidth, reliability, update frequency).
Operator advisory system design and human factors validation (usability, response time, conflict resolution).
Safety Services anomaly detection algorithm and validation data (false positive rate, detection latency).
Network Link redundancy and failover strategy specification.
Customer-defined E_target value and evaluation period.
Baseline energy_per_ton data from similar equipment or pilot operations.
Operator training and incentive alignment plan.
